spec_match.rationale: Draw-then-replay pattern that surfaces offsetX/Y per event in the event log — demonstrates the Chrome 130 preservation.

interactive.rationale: Two side-by-side canvases, draw on one, replay on the other, event log shows each dispatched PointerEvent's coordinates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises draw + replay + log showing preserved offsetX/Y — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Replay now emits a pass/fail verdict comparing recorded offsets to observed untrusted replay event offsets and target state.

design_system.rationale: Replay canvas stroke is sourced from the computed --accent-blue design token, with no raw color literal in the demo code.

summary: Verified in chrome-devtools-mcp with Headless Chrome Canary 151 on 2026-06-06: loaded /v130/coalesced-predicted-events-in-untrusted-pointerevents-will-retain-original-targe/event-replay-inspector/, clicked Clear, synthesized a seven-point canvas gesture, clicked Replay, confirmed the replay canvas drew pixels, and observed the new replay verdict report PASS for all 6 untrusted replay events preserving target and offset coordinates within 1 canvas pixel. The replay stroke is sourced from --accent-blue, the ChromeStatus link is present, and the console and failed-request list were clean.
